meta.name: Command Injection
meta.description: OS command injection payloads for Linux and Windows. Covers basic delimiters, substitution syntax, blind OOB detection, WAF bypass techniques, and Windows-specific variants.

payloads.description: Semicolon delimiter — appends id command after existing command
Pipe delimiter — passes stdout to id command
Background operator — runs id concurrently
OR operator — executes id only if previous command fails
AND operator — executes id only if previous command succeeds
Full chained probe — works in parameters expecting an IP/hostname
Pipe-chained probe for ping-style parameters
AND chain reads /etc/passwd if ping succeeds
Semicolon chain — lists root directory contents
Backtick command substitution — executes id inline
Dollar-paren command substitution — modern bash form
Dollar substitution for arbitrary file read
Internal Field Separator substitution — avoids literal space character
Blind time-based probe — 5 second response delay confirms injection
Blind ping probe — ~5 second delay observable in response time
Blind OOB via curl — confirms RCE with HTTP callback to attacker server
Blind OOB via wget — alternative callback when curl is unavailable
Blind DNS-only OOB — works when HTTP egress is blocked
Blind reverse shell — confirms exec AND provides interactive shell
Brace expansion space bypass — avoids space character entirely
$@ null variable — expands to nothing, breaks keyword pattern matching
Empty string mid-word — 'l''s' → 'ls', breaks simple string filters
Base64-encoded 'id' decoded at runtime — bypasses static keyword detection
Escaped path separators — splits /bin/id with backslashes
Hex-escaped command — \x69\x64 decodes to 'id' at runtime
Windows basic ampersand injection — appends whoami
Windows pipe injection — passes output to whoami
Windows directory listing via dir — confirms code execution
Windows file read — reads win.ini to confirm injection
Windows local user enumeration via net user
